title: [lichen_l2.c:1134-1139] rx_link_key copied but has_link_key not checked for copy
description:
Gap found: Link key copy logic not fully traced.

Why this was missed: Key handling logic reviewed partially, not complete path.

The actual bug: Lines 1134-1139 copy link_key only if has_link_key is true:
  if (link_ctx.has_link_key) {
      memcpy(rx_link_key, link_ctx.link_key, LICHEN_LINK_KEY_LEN);
      rx_link_key_ptr = rx_link_key;
  }

If has_link_key is false, rx_link_key_ptr remains NULL (initialized on line 1135). This is passed to rx_ctx.link_key (line 1158).

The question: what does lichen_link_rx() do when link_key is NULL?
- If it requires link_key for MIC validation, the frame will fail
- If it falls back to CRC32 validation, the frame may be accepted without crypto

This is by design - link_key is optional during initial handshake. The code correctly propagates the key state.

However: if has_link_key is false but an attacker sends a frame with MIC that happens to pass CRC32, it would be accepted. This is documented behavior for unauthenticated bootstrap.

Priority 3 - security design note for unauthenticated mode.
close_reason: Fixed: Added security documentation explaining behavior when has_link_key is false. The code already correctly checks has_link_key before copying (line 1349). Enhanced comment now documents that NULL link_key causes: AES-CCM-64 MIC to fail with -LICHEN_EAUTH; CRC32 MIC to fall back to non-cryptographic verification. This is expected during unauthenticated bootstrap.
